title: The Jersey Stack — Episode 1: The Transistor

series.name: The Jersey Stack
series.thesis: New Jersey was the real tech incubator for the world.
planned_episodes: S1E1: The Transistor (Bell Labs Murray Hill, Dec 23 1947)
S1E2: The Switch (TRADIC / first all-transistor computer, Bell Labs Whippany NJ, 1954)
S1E4: The System (Unix + C, Bell Labs Murray Hill, 1969–78)
S1E5: The Diaspora (BSD, GNU, Linux — epilogue)
series.season_2_tease_locked: DEFERRED — per Gate 1 creator directive, Season 2 is not teased in Season 1. Season 2 green-lights only if Season 1 performs. Crawford Hill / Horn Antenna / CMB content held for eventual Season 2 opener.
series.bookend_narrative: Netflix's $1B, 292-acre, 12-soundstage East Coast production hub at the former Fort Monmouth Army base. Closed Dec 5 2025. Phase 1A opens 2027, Phase 1B 2028. The same soil that invented the transistor is now building the studio where the content streams back. Plus: the Transistor Monument at Holmdel / Bell Works, now famous as Lumon HQ in Severance.

topic: December 23, 1947. Bardeen, Brattain, and Shockley at Bell Labs Murray Hill invent the point-contact transistor. The 20th century pivots. This is the origin story of the Jersey Stack.
logline: December 23, 1947. Three men at Bell Labs in Murray Hill, New Jersey, press a paperclip-sized piece of germanium against two gold contacts. A voice amplifies. The 20th century pivots.
last_updated_note: Post-Gate-2 series thesis sharpening (2026-04-19): series is specifically about transistor→computer. Ep 2 renamed 'The Switch' (TRADIC 1954); PCB/Auto-Sembly demoted from Ep 2 subject to supporting tech in Ep 2/3. Scene 30 narration + visual rewritten to tease amp→switch. Josh re-records of 15 Kokoro scenes complete. Full MP3 reconcat + Scene 30 re-record + Scene 30 image regen pending.

hard_facts_verified.demo_date: December 23, 1947, afternoon, Bell Labs Murray Hill NJ, Lab 1E-455
hard_facts_verified.demo_mechanism: Point-contact transistor: gold foil strips on plastic triangle pressed into germanium slab. Mic -> device -> loudspeaker. Power gain ~18.
hard_facts_verified.bardeen_quote_home: Casual remark to his wife Jane: 'We discovered something today.'
hard_facts_verified.kelly_solid_state_group: Mervin Kelly formed new solid-state group 1945, led by Shockley and chemist Stanley Morgan.
hard_facts_verified.shockley_junction_transistor: January 23, 1948 — Shockley conceived the junction transistor alone in a Chicago hotel room over four weeks, furious at being left off the point-contact patent.
hard_facts_verified.consent_decree: 1956 AT&T consent decree forced licensing of ~7,820 Bell patents including the transistor. Gordon Moore called it 'one of the most important developments for the commercial semiconductor industry.'
hard_facts_verified.traitorous_eight: Sept 18, 1957 — Blank, Grinich, Hoerni, Kleiner, Last, Moore, Noyce, Roberts resigned from Shockley Semiconductor and founded Fairchild. Fairchild spawned Intel (1968, Noyce + Moore), AMD, National Semiconductor, and Kleiner Perkins.
hard_facts_verified.nobel_1956: Shockley, Bardeen, Brattain shared the 1956 Nobel Prize in Physics.
hard_facts_verified.bardeen_two_nobels: Bardeen is the only person to win the Nobel in Physics twice (1956 transistor; 1972 BCS theory of superconductivity).
hard_facts_verified.prc_25: Gen. Creighton Abrams called the transistorized AN/PRC-25 'the single most important tactical item in Vietnam.'
hard_facts_verified.bell_works: Saarinen-designed Holmdel complex completed 1962. Somerset Development bought it 2013 for $27M, reopened as 'Bell Works' mixed-use. Water tower on site is shaped like a transistor (installed ~1961/62). Currently serves as the Lumon HQ exterior/atrium in Apple TV+'s Severance.
hard_facts_verified.netflix_fort_monmouth: Dec 5, 2025 — Netflix closed on 292-acre Fort Monmouth parcel for $55M. $1B+ total investment, 12 soundstages, Phase 1A opens 2027, Phase 1B 2028. Public groundbreaking May 2025; deal closing Dec 5, 2025.
hard_facts_verified.apple_m3_max_transistors: 92 billion transistors (largest publicly-confirmed M-series figure at script time).
hard_facts_verified.crawford_hill: Penzias & Wilson detected the cosmic microwave background at the Crawford Hill Horn Antenna (Holmdel Township, NJ) in 1964. Nobel 1978. Transistorized receiver electronics with cryogenic ruby maser front end made the detection possible. Same township as Bell Works. [HELD FOR POSSIBLE SEASON 2 — not used in Ep 1.]

flagged_for_fact_check: Brief references '~200B transistors on an Apple M-series chip.' Script uses the verified figure (~92B on M3 Max).
Exact attendees at the Dec 23 1947 afternoon demo not enumerated in public sources beyond Bardeen, Brattain, and H. R. Moore plus 'colleagues and managers.'
'Traitorous Eight' naming is a well-known informal label but not a contemporaneous term.
General Creighton Abrams 'single most important tactical item in Vietnam' attribution to PRC-25 is widely cited.
